vBRIEFInfo.description: Scope vBRIEF ingested from GitHub issue #1364

plan.title: swarm skill: poller completion can surface Phase 5→6 merge gate while PRs remain unclean (confidence=3 holdouts)

narratives.Description: swarm skill: poller completion can surface Phase 5→6 merge gate while PRs remain unclean (confidence=3 holdouts). The monitor currently keys the transition to merge discussion on sub-agent lifecycle completion rather than on objective cohort-wide CLEAN state, so a poller that exits with clean_gate_holdout=confidence still lets the orchestrator raise the merge gate.
narratives.Overview:
## Problem

During the #1166 strategy consistency swarm, after spawning canonical poller sub-agents (via templates/swarm-greptile-poller-prompt.md), the monitor moved to "suggest next step" and presented the Phase 5→6 merge-cascade gate recommendation even though multiple PRs had not reached the skill's own strict exit criteria:

- Several pollers exited with confidence=3 holdout (explicitly reported via clean_gate_holdout=confidence).
- At least one was left in "awaiting re-review" state after a fix batch.
- One worktree still had uncommitted changes from the poller batch.
- No PR had a final "PR #N CLEAN -- ready for merge" with confidence > 3 on the current HEAD.

The orchestrator treated "all pollers have reported back" as sufficient to discuss merging.

## Root Cause

The skills/deft-directive-swarm/SKILL.md correctly documents a strong Phase 5 Exit Condition (all PRs must simultaneously have confidence > 3 + no P0/P1 + task check + CHANGELOG) and a mandatory Phase 5→6 gate.

However, when using the post-#727 pattern of short-lived poller sub-agents (Phase 6), there is no *mandatory deterministic gate* the monitor must pass after the pollers terminate before it is allowed to present or discuss the merge decision.

The pollers correctly implement the full triple-tier detector + evaluate_clean_gate + clean_gate_holdout instrumentation (#1039) and can exit on several non-CLEAN terminal conditions (TIMEOUT, STALL, "awaiting re-review", one-shot snapshots, etc.). The skill currently keys the transition to merge discussion on sub-agent lifecycle completion rather than on objective cohort-wide CLEAN state.

Existing per-PR infrastructure (scripts/pr_merge_readiness.py + task pr:merge-ready) is excellent but is only invoked later, per-merge in Phase 6 Step 1 — too late to prevent the monitor from offering the merge gate in the first place.

## Proposed Deterministic Solutions (ranked)

**1. Mandatory cohort-level script gate (strongest, recommended)**

Add `task swarm:verify-review-clean` (new `scripts/swarm_verify_review_clean.py` that re-uses the parser from `pr_merge_readiness.py`).

- Takes list of PRs (or the active vBRIEF cohort).
- Requires *every* PR to pass full CLEAN (SHA match + confidence > 3 + zero P0/P1 + no errored + CI clean) on current HEAD.
- Three-state exit (0 = clean, 1 = blocked with per-PR diagnostics, 2 = error).
- Mandate in swarm skill Phase 5: after pollers report, the monitor **MUST** run this command and **MUST NOT** present the Phase 5→6 gate until it exits 0.

**2. Strengthen poller terminal contract**

Declare in the swarm skill and poller template that, for swarm-orchestrated cycles, the *only* acceptable "review complete" terminal message from a poller is the exact (1) CLEAN subject line with confidence > 3 on current HEAD. All other exits require either fresh poller re-dispatch or explicit user escalation *before* any merge discussion is allowed.

**3. Explicit post-poller barrier in skill + monitor prompt**

Add a required "CLEAN report collection" sub-step in Phase 5. The monitor must have received and logged a distinct CLEAN message for every PR at the final HEAD before the Phase 5→6 gate conversation is reachable.

**4. Minor supporting changes**

- Mark one-shot/limited poller modes as non-authoritative for merge readiness.
- Consider a small `.deft-scratch` collection file for auditability of CLEAN reports.

## Why deterministic matters here

This is exactly the class of failure the framework has worked hard to close elsewhere (task verify:* gates, #810 preflight, #1039 clean_gate_holdout instrumentation, pr_merge_readiness.py, etc.). The swarm skill should not be the exception.

The fix should make it *structurally difficult* for a monitor (human or agent) to propose merging while any PR in the cohort is still below the documented exit bar.

Refs: #1166 swarm execution, poller artifacts showing multiple conf=3 + clean_gate_holdout=confidence exits, existing scripts/pr_merge_readiness.py and templates/swarm-greptile-poller-prompt.md.
narratives.UserStory: As a swarm monitor, I want a cohort-level CLEAN verification gate before the Phase 5→6 merge discussion, so that I cannot surface a merge cascade while any PR is still below the documented exit bar.
narratives.ImplementationPlan:
1. Add scripts/swarm_verify_review_clean.py that accepts a list of PRs (or the active vBRIEF cohort), parses each PR's Greptile rolling-summary using the existing parser from scripts/pr_merge_readiness.py, and exits 0 only when every PR has SHA match, confidence > 3, zero P0/P1, not errored, and CI clean on current HEAD; exits 1 with per-PR diagnostics otherwise, exits 2 on config error.
2. Register task swarm:verify-review-clean (new file tasks/swarm.yml or extend the existing one) that wraps the script with bare {{.CLI_ARGS}} forwarding per the established Taskfile convention.
3. Update skills/deft-directive-swarm/SKILL.md Phase 5 Exit Condition and the Phase 5→6 gate to mandate task swarm:verify-review-clean after pollers report and before any merge discussion; add an anti-pattern that forbids basing the merge gate on poller lifecycle completion alone.
4. Update templates/swarm-greptile-poller-prompt.md to declare that the only acceptable swarm-orchestrated CLEAN terminal message is the exact subject line with confidence > 3 on current HEAD; all other exits require fresh poller re-dispatch or explicit user escalation before merge discussion.
5. Add tests/cli/test_swarm_verify_review_clean.py covering all-clean cohort (exit 0), confidence=3 holdout (exit 1), SHA-stale HEAD (exit 1), Greptile-errored sentinel (exit 1), and empty cohort or malformed config (exit 2).
6. Add a CHANGELOG.md [Unreleased] entry referencing #1364.
narratives.Traces: Traces to GitHub issue #1364 and the #1166 swarm execution where pollers exited with confidence=3 holdouts. Reuses the parser from scripts/pr_merge_readiness.py and updates templates/swarm-greptile-poller-prompt.md.

narrative.Acceptance: Given a list of PRs, when the script runs, it parses each PR's Greptile rolling-summary on current HEAD and exits 0 only when every PR has SHA match, confidence > 3, zero P0/P1, not errored, and CI clean; otherwise exits 1 with per-PR diagnostics or exits 2 on config error.
narrative.Traces: Traces to GitHub issue #1364 -- swarm:verify-review-clean script with three-state exit.

narrative.Acceptance: When invoked via `task swarm:verify-review-clean -- <args>`, the target forwards CLI_ARGS via the established bare-{{.CLI_ARGS}} convention and surfaces the script's exit code unchanged.
narrative.Traces: Traces to GitHub issue #1364 -- Taskfile target task swarm:verify-review-clean.

items.title: Swarm skill Phase 5→6 gate cites the new verifier

narrative.Acceptance: When all pollers have reported and the monitor reaches the Phase 5→6 gate, the swarm skill rejects any merge discussion until task swarm:verify-review-clean exits 0, and an anti-pattern explicitly blocks keying the transition on poller lifecycle completion alone.
narrative.Traces: Traces to GitHub issue #1364 -- Swarm skill Phase 5→6 gate cites the new verifier.

narrative.Acceptance: When a swarm-orchestrated poller terminates, templates/swarm-greptile-poller-prompt.md rejects any 'review complete' signal other than the CLEAN subject line with confidence > 3 on current HEAD, and any other exit forces fresh poller re-dispatch or explicit user escalation before merge discussion.
narrative.Traces: Traces to GitHub issue #1364 -- Poller template strengthened terminal contract.

items.title: Tests cover clean, blocked, errored, and config-error paths

narrative.Acceptance: When pytest runs tests/cli/test_swarm_verify_review_clean.py, it validates the all-clean cohort path (exit 0), the confidence=3 holdout path (exit 1 with diagnostics), the SHA-stale HEAD path (exit 1), the Greptile-errored sentinel path (exit 1), and the empty-cohort / malformed-config path (exit 2).
narrative.Traces: Traces to GitHub issue #1364 -- Tests cover clean, blocked, errored, and config-error paths.

references.title: Issue #1364: swarm skill: poller completion can surface Phase 5→6 merge gate while PRs remain unclean (confidence=3 holdouts)

expected_outputs: task swarm:verify-review-clean exits 0 on a clean cohort and 1 on any unclean PR with diagnostic body
swarm SKILL.md Phase 5→6 gate references task swarm:verify-review-clean and an anti-pattern forbids merge discussion before exit 0
swarm-greptile-poller-prompt.md restricts swarm-cycle CLEAN terminal message to confidence > 3 on current HEAD
